commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a1cd846cc84d41edb9bd47e5fcfd5bb3dce826e1)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Linux systemd gateway installation detection, doctor cleanup of simultaneous user/system units, service-mode dueling warnings, and regression tests.

PR surface: Source +234, Tests +264. Total +498 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source-level bug: current main's lifecycle detector returns the user unit first when both canonical scopes exist, so the dueling state cannot be surfaced there. I did not live-reproduce the WSL2 systemd restart loop in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor or maintainer revision plus real environment proof; automation cannot supply the contributor's required systemd setup proof for this external PR.

target.title: fix(daemon): detect and resolve dueling user+system systemd gateway units (#79375)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #79375 — on Linux, after an upgrade a **user-scope** unit
(`~/.config/systemd/user/openclaw-gateway.service`) and a **system-scope** unit
(`/etc/systemd/system/openclaw-gateway.service`) can both exist and both try to
manage the gateway. They bind the same port and each instance's stale-process
detection SIGTERMs the other, producing an endless restart cascade.

Root cause: `findInstalledSystemdGatewayScope` checked the user path first and
**returned early**, so it could never surface that both scopes coexist — the
"dueling" condition was structurally invisible.

## What changed (three layers)

**1. Detection (`src/daemon/systemd.ts`)**
- New `findSystemdGatewayInstallation(env)` returns a discriminated union
  `{ kind: "none" | "user" | "system" | "dueling" }` that reports *every*
  installed scope without early-returning. Modeling it as a union (rather than a
  `{user|null, system|null}` pair) keeps the dueling state unambiguous.
- `findInstalledSystemdGatewayScope` is refactored to delegate to it while
  **preserving the exact user-first preference** its four lifecycle callers
  (`stop`/`restart`/`is-enabled`/`runtime`) rely on — no behavior change there.
- New `uninstallUserSystemdGatewayUnit` removes only the `$HOME` user unit (no
  root required), mirroring the existing `uninstallLegacySystemdUnits` pattern.
- New pure `formatDuelingScopesWarning(installation, port)` helper.

**2. Doctor resolution (`src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.ts`)**
- New `maybeResolveDuelingSystemdGatewayScopes` detects the dueling state and,
  after the existing confirm/policy gate (`resolveServiceRepairPolicy` /
  `confirmDoctorServiceRepair`), removes the redundant **user-scope** unit while
  keeping the root-installed **system-scope** unit authoritative — exactly the
  reporter's manual workaround. Declining, or an externally-managed repair
  policy, falls back to the existing `renderGatewayServiceCleanupHints()` hints.
  Wired into the doctor health flow right after `maybeScanExtraGatewayServices`.

**3. Startup guard (`src/cli/gateway-cli/run.ts`)**
- In service mode, *only when a stale-kill actually happened*, log a targeted
  remediation pointing at `openclaw doctor --fix` instead of letting the loop
  look like routine stale cleanup. Diagnostic only — the kill decision is
  unchanged, and the lookup is skipped on clean starts.

### Design notes
- The canonical units are deliberately excluded from `findExtraGatewayServices`,
  so dueling detection is done directly via `findSystemdGatewayInstallation`
  rather than widening the extra-services scan (smaller blast radius).
- The system-scope unit is **never** auto-removed (it needs root); only a
  `sudo`-flavored hint is offered for that direction, consistent with the
  existing `runSystemdServiceAction` refusal.
- No new config keys; reuses `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_REPAIR_POLICY`, `note()`, and the
  existing confirm/policy helpers.

## Real behavior proof

Built and validated against a fresh clone (Linux, Node v26, pnpm 11.2.2).

**`pnpm build`** — succeeded:
```
[build-all] phase timings: total 204.0s; slowest tsdown 133.0s; ...
✓ built in 1.17s
```

**`pnpm check`** — typecheck + lint clean on touched files (full lint run is green after the `consistent-return` fix):
```
22.37s  ok  typecheck prod
$ npx oxlint <touched files + tests>  ->  EXIT=0
```

**Tests** — `131 passed (131)` across the two affected suites:
```
$ npx vitest run src/daemon/systemd.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  131 passed (131)
```
New cases: `findSystemdGatewayInstallation` (none/user/system/dueling),
`formatDuelingScopesWarning` (dueling vs single-scope), `uninstallUserSystemdGatewayUnit`
(systemctl available / ENOENT / systemctl-unavailable), and doctor
`maybeResolveDuelingSystemdGatewayScopes` (confirm / decline / external-policy /
single-scope / non-Linux). The pre-existing user-first regression test is kept
unchanged.

**End-to-end against the COMPILED detector, real fs I/O, no mocks** — a script
that loads `dist/` and runs `findSystemdGatewayInstallation` against a real temp
`$HOME`:
```
A. empty HOME -> {"kind":"none"}
B. real user unit on disk -> {"kind":"user","user":{"scope":"user",
   "unitName":"openclaw-gateway.service","unitPath":".../systemd/user/openclaw-gateway.service"}}
C. dueling warning ->
   detected BOTH a user-scope (.../systemd/user/openclaw-gateway.service) and a
   system-scope (/etc/systemd/system/openclaw-gateway.service) gateway unit bound
   to port 18789; they will SIGTERM each other in a restart loop. Run
   `openclaw doctor --fix` to remove the redundant user-scope unit, or run:
   systemctl --user disable --now openclaw-gateway.service && rm .../openclaw-gateway.service
D. warning for user-only -> null
```

### What I did **not** test
- The full end-to-end dueling resolution with a **real** system-scope unit under
  `/etc/systemd/system` + an interactive `openclaw doctor --fix` run: the system
  path is hardcoded and writing it needs root, which I could not do in this
  environment. That path is covered by the unit tests above (doctor flow with a
  mocked `findSystemdGatewayInstallation` returning `dueling`, asserting the
  user unit is removed and the system unit is untouched).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
